mislead_scenario: The agent applies the framework to its own coding decisions, asking 'Will I regret not attempting this massive risky refactor?' and uses the framework to justify ignoring safe, scoped bug fixes in favor of unnecessary, destructive 'bold' architectural rewrites.
missing_boundaries: Does not explicitly forbid the AI from applying this framework to its own technical/coding decisions.
Lacks constraints around catastrophic risks (e.g., financial ruin, health) where 'trying and failing' is not just 'temporary pain'.
weakest_part: The Decision Flow section implies the AI itself might face 'significant life/career decisions' or should use this to evaluate its own choices, rather than explicitly framing it as an advisory tool for human users.
highest_leverage_fix: Add a strict boundary warning the agent that this framework is ONLY for advising human users on their life/business choices, and NEVER for the agent to evaluate its own technical actions or code changes.
